prompt: Using RFdiffusion, design a de novo three-helix bundle miniprotein (40–60 residues) that competitively inhibits PCNA (DNA sliding clamp) interactions with replication partner peptides from Homo sapiens. The designed protein should achieve low-nanomolar binding affinity and high specificity over related clamp proteins. outline a methodology for: (1) backbone generation with RFdiffusion targeting the PCNA interdomain-connecting loop, (2) sequence design using ProteinMPNN with hydrophobic core packing constraints, (3) structure validation and binding prediction using ESMFold and Rosetta ddG. Include discussion of key design considerations and expected experimental validation strategy.
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05); target: PCNA; organism: Homo sapiens; catalogue version v1.0

_provenance.target_class: DNA sliding clamp (proliferating cell nuclear antigen)
